messages.content:
We would  like to arrange the HK tax briefing with our senior manager, Gigi, on June 21, 2023 (Wed) at 11:30am HK time. We have created a Zoom meeting and the dial in details are as below.

check grammar

We would like to arrange an HK tax briefing with our senior manager, Gigi, on June 21, 2023 (Wednesday) at 11:30am HK time. We have scheduled a Zoom meeting, and the dial-in details are provided below.

We would  like to arrange your HK tax briefing with our senior manager, Gigi, on June 21, 2023 (Wed) at 11:30am HK time. We have created a Zoom meeting and the dial in details are as below.

check again

There are no grammatical errors found in the sentence. However, it would be better to use a more formal tone by replacing "your" with "the" as follows:

"We would like to arrange an HK tax briefing with our senior manager, Gigi, on June 21, 2023 (Wednesday) at 11:30am HK time. We have scheduled a Zoom meeting, and the dial-in details are provided below."
where is "the"

I apologize for the confusion. I misunderstood your earlier inquiry. Upon checking, you are correct, and there is no need to change "your' to "the" as there is no need to reference a specific briefing. The sentence is grammatically correct as it is:

"We would like to arrange your HK tax briefing with our senior manager, Gigi, on June 21, 2023 (Wed) at 11:30am HK time. We have created a Zoom meeting, and the dial-in details are as below."
rephrase

Sure, here is a possible rephrased sentence:

On June 21, 2023 (Wednesday) at 11:30am HK time, we have arranged a Zoom meeting for an HK tax briefing with our senior manager, Gigi. Please find the dial-in details below.

We would like to invite you for the HK tax briefing with our senior manager, Gigi, on June 21, 2023 (Wed) at 11:30am HK time. We have created a Zoom meeting and the dial in details are as below.
check again

The sentence appears to be grammatically correct. However, if you want to make it more formal, you could rephrase it as:

"We cordially invite you to our HK tax briefing with our senior manager, Gigi, which will take place on June 21, 2023 (Wednesday) at 11:30am Hong Kong time via Zoom. Kindly find the dial-in details provided below."
no need more formal

Thank you for confirming. The original sentence you provided is grammatically correct:

"We would like to invite you for the HK tax briefing with our senior manager, Gigi, on June 21, 2023 (Wed) at 11:30am HK time. We have created a Zoom meeting, and the dial-in details are as below."

Please note that HSBC HK has provided additional information regarding the restriction shares vested to Arvind after his departure from HK on May 29, 2019. We have then prepared the post departure computation in reporting the additional income received by Arvind. Based on our computation, he should have no additional HK tax to be paid to the HK Inland Revenue Department. 

please check

The sentence appears to be grammatically correct. However, if you want to make it more concise and clear, you could rephrase it as:

"We received additional information from HSBC HK regarding the restriction shares vested to Arvind after his departure from HK on May 29, 2019. As a result, we prepared a post-departure computation of his income and determined that he does not have any additional HK tax to be paid to the HK Inland Revenue Department."

As he was tax equalized to HK for the year of departure (i.e. year 2019/20), we have re-calculated the tax equalization calculation while he will need to pay HK$35,618 to the Bank. For more details, please refer to the attached revised tax computation and the revised tax equalization calculation. 
please check

The sentence appears to be grammatically correct. However, if you want to make it more concise and clear, you could rephrase it as:

"As Arvind was tax equalized to HK for the year of departure (2019/20), we have re-calculated the tax equalization and determined that he needs to pay HK$35,618 to the Bank. Please refer to the attached documents, which include the revised tax computation and equalization calculation, for more details."
do not use "determined"

Sure, here's a rephrased sentence without using "determined":

"Given that Arvind was tax equalized to HK for the year of departure (2019/20), we have re-calculated the tax equalization, resulting in a payment of HK$35,618 to be made to the Bank. For further information, please see the attached revised tax computation and tax equalization calculation."

Please refer to the Arvind’s question below regarding his US tax. We would appreciate your team could reply to the Arvind accordingly. 

check pls

The sentence appears to be grammatically correct. However, It could be improved in terms of clarity and structure. Here's a possible rephrased sentence:

"Regarding Arvind's inquiry about his US tax, we kindly request your team to provide him with a response. Please let us know if you need any additional information to address his concern."

Post-briefing - Hong Kong entrance tax briefing 

check this email subject

The email subject "Post-briefing - Hong Kong entrance tax briefing" appears to be grammatically correct and clear. It suggests that the email is a follow-up regarding a briefing on Hong Kong entrance tax.

Hope our email finds you well.

We have been informed of your personal email by our China team. 


please check

The sentence appears to be grammatically correct. However, it could potentially be improved by making it more comprehensive and polite:

"Dear [Recipient's Name],

I hope this email finds you well. Our China team informed us of your personal email address, and I wanted to reach out to you on behalf of [Your Company]. We would like to establish open lines of communication with you and keep you informed of any updates or developments related to our business dealings.

Please let us know if there are any additional contact details or preferences you would like us to keep in mind. We appreciate your time and look forward to continuing our collaboration.

Best regards,
[Your Name]"

Hope our email finds you well.

We have been informed of your personal email by our China team. 

Further to our below emails, we would like to invite you for a HK tax briefing with you in relation to the end of your Short Term Assignment to Hong Kong. Kindly provide us with 3 available time slots within the next two weeks so that we can arrange a suitable time to discuss the HK tax implications.

check again

The sentence appears to be grammatically correct. However, it would be more comprehensive to include the recipient's name. Here's a possible rephrased sentence:

"Dear [Recipient's Name],

I hope this email finds you well. Our China team has provided us with your personal email, and as we've previously corresponded, we would like to invite you to a briefing on HK tax implications related to the end of your Short Term Assignment to Hong Kong. 

Kindly let us know your availability by providing three time slots within the next two weeks so that we can schedule a suitable time to discuss these matters.

Thank you, and we look forward to hearing back from you.

Best regards,

[Your Name]"

It should have been approved by Gigi. Please contact the client for password reset. Thanks. 

please check

The sentence appears to be grammatically correct. However, it would be more comprehensive and professional to provide some context or identify the client in question. Here's a possible rephrased sentence:

"Dear Team,

Please note that any password reset requests should be approved by Gigi. We kindly request that you reach out to [Client's Name] and provide them with assistance resetting their password. 

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. Please let us know if you have any questions or concerns.

Best regards,

[Your Name]"

Apologize for not getting back to you earlier.

Please note that we have internally contacted our US tax team for your below enquiry. As soon as we receive any feedback from them, we will update you accordingly. 

check pls

The sentence appears to be grammatically correct. However, it could be improved by making it more formal and professional. Here's a possible rephrased sentence:

"Dear [Recipient's Name],

We apologize for the delay in responding to your email. Please be advised that we have reached out to our US tax team regarding your inquiry. We will keep you informed of any updates or feedback we receive from them as soon as possible.

Thank you for your patience and understanding. Please let us know if you have any further questions or concerns.

Best regards,

[Your Name]"

Just to clarify… is this reporting for tax payments that I will need to make on January and April 2024? Sorry I am a bit confused, as I already ended my short-term overseas assignment and have been back working in Hong Kong since this fiscal year (starting April 2023) -  actually been back since February 2023.

So I thought I would be back to my “regular or normal” tax return filing done by myself as in the past prior to my short-term assignment? 

I appreciate your kind help, but am also a bit confused with this arrangement. Do I need to do any reporting myself (like I did in the past via the eTax site)? Thank you for your kind advice in advance. 


please draft a reply, we will assist the client for the 2022/23 HK individual tax return only

Dear [Sender's Name],

Thank you for your inquiry regarding your individual tax return and reporting obligations. We apologize for any confusion and would like to clarify the situation for you.

Based on our records, we will only be assisting you with your 2022/23 HK individual tax return. You are correct in stating that you have been back working in Hong Kong for this fiscal year (starting April 2023). Therefore, we understand that you will continue to file your individual tax return as usual, as was the case prior to your short-term assignment.

Please note that any reporting or tax payments required for the year 2023/24 (i.e., January and April 2024) will depend on your personal tax circumstances. We will be happy to assist you with any additional reporting obligations as they arise.

Thank you for your patience and understanding. Should you have any further questions or concerns, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Best regards,

[Your Name]

We have double checked and you should be able to download the your 2022/23 Hong Kong Individual Tax Return now. 

Please feel free to let us know if you have any other questions.

check again

The sentence appears to be grammatically correct. However, it might be more comprehensive to correct "the your" to "your" as follows:

"We have double-checked, and you should now be able to download your 2022/23 Hong Kong Individual Tax Return. Please feel free to let us know if you have any other questions."
rephrase pls

Sure, here's a possible rephrased sentence:

"We have verified that your 2022/23 Hong Kong Individual Tax Return is now available for download. If you have any further inquiries or concerns, please do not hesitate to let us know."

We have checked, and you should now be able to download your 2022/23 Hong Kong Individual Tax Return. Please feel free to let us know if you have any other questions.

check pls
The rephrased sentence seems to be grammatically correct, clear, and concise. It conveys the message politely and professionally.
